alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Allergen List - Allergen declaration on labels of prepackaged foodstuffs
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against GSO 9:2022.

alerts.details: New: Verification required. The Food Safety Basic Law provides the fundamental principles for ensuring food safety, which includes additive control.

alerts.details: Old: Verification required against Specifications and Standards. New: Verification required. General information on food additives, specific limits would be in detailed regulations.

alerts.details: New: Verification required. This document contains detailed specifications and standards for food additives.

alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Non-permitted food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Do not import or sell food products containing non-permitted additives. An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of a non-permitted additive.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Do not import or sell food products containing contaminants exceeding regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Diethylstibestrol, Hexoestrol, Dienoestrol
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Prohibition is under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Ingredients and additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels. Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Labelling
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must comply with general labelling requirements.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Additive Limits - γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific use restrictions for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified against the full regulation.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10 or more. Food allergen labeling is required for prepackaged foods containing these allergens as an ingredient or food additive. For sulphite, labeling is required if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Labelling Requirement - γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient must be verified against the full regulation.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited Foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibition. Certain foods are explicitly prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification is required to identify these specific foods.

alerts.title: Modified: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited from use in food (21 CFR Part 189). Verification required.. New: Prohibited from use. Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. Sesame was added effective January 1, 2023.

alerts.title: Modified: United States Contaminant Limits - 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: 1. New: 1. This is a guidance level.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an action level.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Various chemical contaminants (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations. The tool provides a consolidated list of contaminant levels including tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - 'Gluten-free' claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Meets defined standard. Foods labeled 'gluten-free' must meet a defined standard for gluten content. Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (effective 2020-08-12).

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen preventive controls
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance with 21 CFR part 117. Facilities must implement allergen preventive controls as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) to prevent allergen cross-contact and undeclared allergens, including written procedures.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. The food source of all major food allergens must be declared. This includes the specific type of tree nut, fish, and Crustacean shellfish. Declaration can be in parentheses after the ingredient or in a 'contains' statement immediately after or next to the ingredient list.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting agents (e.g., sodium bisulfite)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10. Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 ppm total sulfites.

alerts.title: Modified: China Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required per GB 2760-2015. New: Verification required against GB 2760-2015. Specific limits and conditions for food additive use must be verified against the full text of GB 2760-2015.

alerts.title: Removed: China Additive Limits - Food Additive Limits
alerts.details: Old: Verification of relevant GB Food Safety Standards is required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: China Allergen List - Allergen Declaration
alerts.details: Old: Verification of relevant GB Food Safety Standards is required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: China Contaminant Limits - Contaminant Limits
alerts.details: Old: Verification of relevant GB Food Safety Standards is required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: China Labelling Requirement - Food Safety Standards
alerts.details: New: Verification required against applicable GB Food Safety Standards. The National Health Commission is responsible for food safety standards. Verification of applicable GB Food Safety Standards is required for all food safety parameters, including but not limited to additive limits, microbiology limits, contaminant limits, salt/sodium limits, allergen declaration/list, and labelling warnings.

alerts.title: Removed: China Labelling Requirement - Labelling Requirements
alerts.details: Old: Verification of relevant GB Food Safety Standards is required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: China Microbiology Limits - Microbiology Limits
alerts.details: Old: Verification of relevant GB Food Safety Standards is required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: China Salt / Sodium Limits - Salt / Sodium Limits
alerts.details: Old: Verification of relevant GB Food Safety Standards is required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. New: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). New: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels. Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins in Spices
alerts.details: New: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction. A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) is available for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins
alerts.details: Old: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. The use of food additives and their conditions of use in the EU are governed by the Union list of food additives, detailed in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008. Specific limits and conditions must be verified through the Additives Database.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 establishes food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins, and metabolites in specific foods. Specific limits and conditions must be verified within the regulation.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Modified: Indonesia Additive Limits - Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. New: Verification required for production, import, registration, and circulation. Specific regulations apply to the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific limits for food additives in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, are regulated by BPOM. Refer to the regulations on 'Pangan Olahan' and 'PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan' for detailed requirements.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Allergen List - Allergen declaration
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Requirements for allergen declaration in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, are regulated by BPOM. Refer to the regulations on 'Pangan Olahan' for detailed requirements.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, pesticides)
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific contaminant limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, are regulated by BPOM. Refer to the regulations on 'Pangan Olahan' for detailed requirements.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Labelling Requirement - Labelling warnings and general requirements
alerts.details: New: Verification required. General labelling requirements and warnings for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, are regulated by BPOM. Refer to the regulations on 'Pangan Olahan' for detailed requirements.

alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific microbiological limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, are regulated by BPOM. Refer to the regulations on 'Pangan Olahan' for detailed requirements.

alerts.title: Added: Indonesia Salt / Sodium Limits - Salt / Sodium
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific salt/sodium limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, are regulated by BPOM. Refer to the regulations on 'Pangan Olahan' for detailed requirements.

alerts.details: Old: Verification required against the Positive List System. New: Verification required. The 'Positive List System' implies a detailed list of limits for agricultural chemical residues.
